steps.title: Find the clue in the passage
steps.content: Hardy writes that seasonal changes are “less obtrusive” on open land than in woodland, yet “just as perceptible” to a close observer. The contrast between “less obtrusive” and “just as perceptible” shows that obtrusive describes how easily noticeable or prominent something is.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Stubborn: Stubborn means refusing to change — this relates to persistence, not how visually noticeable something is. ✗
  • Choice B — Expansive: Expansive means wide-ranging or broad — this describes size or scope, not how prominent something is to an observer. ✗
  • Choice C — Insolent: Insolent means rude or disrespectful — this is a personality trait with no relation to seasonal visibility. ✗
  • Choice D — Conspicuous: Conspicuous means easily noticed or standing out — seasons are less conspicuous (less obtrusive) on open land, which fits the contrast with “just as perceptible.” ✓
That confirms D is correct.
